Order Custom Patches
Expert Technical Guide

The Ultimate Backing Guide

Selecting the right backing is just as important as the design. Whether you are branding heavy jackets or delicate costumes, we have the perfect technical solution.

Heat Seal (Iron-On)

Best For: Varsity Jackets, Hoodies, Cotton T-Shirts

Our most popular choice. A specialized adhesive is applied to the back of the patch that bonds permanently to the fabric when heat is applied with a heat press or home iron.

Stacy's Tip: While the bond is permanent, we always recommend a 'tack stitch' on the corners for garments that undergo heavy athletic movement.

Technical Photo: Heat Seal (Iron-On)

Traditional Sew-On

Best For: Delicate Costumes, Mesh, Lycra, Performance Silks

The cleanest finish for performance gear. Sew-on patches have no adhesive, making them soft and flexible so they don't 'stiffen' the garment during high-energy dance routines.

Best for costumes that need to be washed frequently or fabrics that can't handle high heat.

Technical Photo: Traditional Sew-On

Velcro (Hook & Loop)

Best For: Tactical Gear, Team Bags, Swappable Award Systems

Includes both the hook (on the patch) and the loop (to be sewn to the garment). Perfect for studios that want to swap out competition year patches or merit awards.

Great for gear bags where you want to rotate different style patches throughout the season.

Technical Photo: Velcro (Hook & Loop)

Peel & Stick (Self-Adhesive)

Best For: One-Day Events, Recitals, Temporary Awards

Works like a high-end sticker. Perfect for temporary placement. Note: This is not a permanent solution for laundry; it is designed for short-term flair.

Ideal for student gift bags at recitals or summer intensives.

Technical Photo: Peel & Stick (Self-Adhesive)

Keychain / Bag Tag

Best For: Dance Bags, Keyrings, Studio Merchandise

We add a metal ring and grommet to your patch, turning your studio logo into a high-end accessory that students can hang on their bags.

One of the highest-selling merchandise items for dance studios.

Technical Photo: Keychain / Bag Tag

Application & Care

Everything you need to know about making your patches last.

How do I apply Heat Seal (Iron-on) patches?

Set your heat press to 350°F (175°C) or your iron to the 'Cotton' setting. Apply firm pressure for 15-20 seconds on the front, then flip the garment and apply pressure for another 10-15 seconds on the back to ensure the adhesive melts deep into the fibers.

Can I wash garments with patches?

Yes! We recommend washing inside out on a cold, gentle cycle. Air drying is always best to preserve the vibrancy of the threads and the integrity of the backing adhesive.

Ready to Get Started?
